page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an AMD EPYC 7H12 running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is poor, placing in the bottom 10% with a score of 767.49. However, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels, scoring 6127.52. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a similar divergence: cached small-block reads achieve an average speed of 802.27 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block reads and writes are weak, registering at 30.52 GB/sec and 15.5 GB/sec respectively. Other metrics, such as PassMark CPU score (19093.04) and Redis SET operations (14.17 million operations per second), consistently fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a stable resource density for standard workloads without the premium of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The primary tradeoff is the weak single-core execution speed and uncached memory throughput, which may bottleneck serialized tasks. Consequently, this instance is best suited for multi-threaded general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and moderate Redis deployments that can leverage the 16 dedicated cores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM, a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage. Benchmark data shows a contrast between poor single-core CPU performance (bottom 10%) and average multi-core capabilities, alongside weak uncached memory bandwidth but average cached read speeds. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a stable resource profile for multi-threaded workloads. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations that do not rely heavily on high single-thread execution speeds.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core instance with a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, optimized for parallelized general-purpose workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores and vCPUs
64.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded applications
